content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Luke E. Williams, Jr. 
Sweeney Fellow 
The Fels Center of Government 
P.O. Box 42228 
Philadelphia, PA 
19104 
Dear Mr. Williams: 
November 28, 1988 
Re: 
Our File No. G-88-445 
You have requested current regulations on starting a PAC 
and campaign committee in California. 
Enclosed is a copy of 
the Political Reform Actli and Propositions 68 and 73, which 
were recently adopted by the voters as amendment to the 
Political Reform Act. 
The Commission believes that most 
provisions of Propositions 68 and 73 conflict. 
Proposition 73 
passed with a greater number of affirmative votes; therefore, 
it prevails over proposition 68, to the extent that conflicts 
exist. 
(In re Bell (1988) 11 FPPC Ops. 1, copy enclosed.) 
Also enclosed is a copy of the 1988 manual on campaign 
disclosure and recently adopted Regulations 18536-18536.2, 
which implement a portion of Proposition 73. 
We anticipate 
that our 1989 manual on campaign disclosure and a manual on 
Propositions 68 and 73 will be available by late January 1989. 
If you have any specific questions concerning the 
requirements for starting a PAC in California, please contact 
the Commission's Technical Assistance and Analysis Division at 
(916) 322-5662. 
KED:plh 
Enclosures 
sincerely, 
i<a.n~LV)fV"- t ' '0t--vU1-"7t~ 
Kathryn ~. Donovan 
Acting General Counsel 
li Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code 
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. 
All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code 
of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660

sections.question_synthetic: What are the current regulations for starting a PAC and campaign committee in California, particularly in light of the recently adopted Propositions 68 and 73?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provided regulatory guidance materials and clarified that Proposition 73 prevails over Proposition 68 where conflicts exist, as it received a greater number of affirmative votes. The Commission directed the requester to contact the Technical Assistance and Analysis Division for specific PAC formation questions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks traditional 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' section headers. Question and conclusion were synthesized from letter content. Response is informational/guidance-focused rather than opining on specific conduct. Document references enclosures but text is primary substantive content.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the current regulations for starting a PAC and campaign committee in California, particularly in light of the recently adopted Propositions 68 and 73?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC provided regulatory guidance materials and clarified that Proposition 73 prevails over Proposition 68 where conflicts exist, as it received a greater number of affirmative votes. The Commission directed the requester to contact the Technical Assistance and Analysis Division for specific PAC formation questions.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to a request for current regulations on starting a PAC and campaign committee in California by providing copies of the Political Reform Act, Propositions 68 and 73, campaign disclosure manuals, and recently adopted regulations, with clarification on the relative precedence of the two propositions.
